articles.title: Ethiopia Nears Restructuring Deal with Bondholders
articles.summary: The Ministry of Finance announced today it has reached an agreement in principle with an Ad Hoc Committee representing investors behind its one-billion-dollar Eurobond. The agreement, which features terms for subscription rights for a new international bond to be issued in the future, is the second such deal between Ethiopia and bondholders this year, and […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020 and has strained its finances. The government’s previous restructuring deal in March 2023 aimed to alleviate debt burdens, but ongoing tensions and a need for international investment have made this new agreement with bondholders crucial for stabilizing the economy and fostering investor confidence amid a backdrop of political unrest and humanitarian crises.

articles.title: اتفاق تعاون بين جامعة الجزيرة ومؤسسة مدارك القطرية
articles.summary: وقّعت جامعة الجزيرة ومؤسسة مدارك للبحوث والخدمات الجامعية بدولة قطر عبر تقنية الإتصال المرئي (Google Meet) صباح اليوم إتفاقية تعاون أكاديمي لتعزيز التعاون الأكاديمي والترويج لبرامج الدراسات العليا بكلية الإعلام على المستويين الإقليمي والدولي . حيث وقّع عن جامعة الجزيرة الدكتور ربيع أحمد أسد عميد كلية الدراسات العليا ووقّع عن مؤسسة مدارك البروفيسور إبراهيم أحمد …

articles.title: الشمالية: 90% نسبة أداء التأمين الصحي خلال النصف الأول من 2026

articles.context: تأتي هذه الإحصائيات في وقت حرج للسودان، حيث يعاني من صراعات داخلية مستمرة منذ عام 2019 بعد الإطاحة بنظام البشير. تساهم هذه الإنجازات في التأمين الصحي في تعزيز الاستقرار الاجتماعي والاقتصادي في ولاية الشمالية، التي تأثرت بشدة من النزاعات. كما أن تحسين التغطية الصحية يتماشى مع جهود الحكومة الانتقالية لتعزيز الخدمات العامة واستعادة الثقة بين المواطنين.

articles.context: تواجه السودان موجات حر شديدة هذا الصيف، مما يزيد من التحديات الصحية في ظل الأزمات الإنسانية المستمرة. النزاع المستمر منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع أدى إلى تفاقم الظروف المعيشية، مما يجعل الوصول إلى المياه النظيفة والرعاية الصحية أكثر صعوبة. في هذا السياق، يصبح الحفاظ على برودة الجسم أمرًا حيويًا لتجنب المخاطر الصحية المرتبطة بالحرارة.

This move by President Kiir may be an attempt to consolidate control and address local grievances as the country approaches the 2024 elections.

articles.title: SPLM-IO questions election timetable, cites unmet peace deal provisions
articles.summary: A faction of the opposition SPLM-IO loyal to detained First Vice President Riek Machar questioned The post SPLM-IO questions election timetable, cites unmet peace deal provisions appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The SPLM-IO, led by Riek Machar, has been a key player in South Sudan's turbulent political landscape since the civil war erupted in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace and a transitional government, yet many provisions remain unfulfilled, fueling tensions. With elections scheduled for 2024, the SPLM-IO's concerns about the timeline highlight ongoing instability and the fragile nature of the peace process.

articles.title: Activist calls for establishment of affordable rehab centers in all states
articles.summary: A women’s rights advocate has called for the establishment of affordable rehabilitation centers in all the states and three administrative areas to help address rising drug abuse among young people. […] The post Activist calls for establishment of affordable rehab centers in all states appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with the aftermath of its civil war, which officially ended in 2018 with the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan. Despite the peace deal, ongoing instability and economic challenges have exacerbated youth unemployment and drug abuse. The South Sudan Women Empowerment Network, led by Adut Christina, aims to address these pressing issues as the government struggles to implement effective social services amidst the political turmoil.

articles.title: Run Where the Equator Meets the Mountains of the Moon

As the country seeks to rebuild and foster unity, participation in international events like this marathon can enhance Ethiopia's image and promote regional cooperation in the Horn of Africa, particularly in sports diplomacy.

articles.title: TPLF Rejects EU Criticism, Accuses Federal Government of Violating Peace Deal
articles.summary: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) has rejected European Union accusations that it is undermining Ethiopia’s 2022 peace agreement, instead accusing the federal government of violating the accord and warning that the Tigray region faces a growing risk of renewed conflict. In a statement issued in response to the European Union delegation’s call for the […]
articles.context: The TPLF's rejection of EU criticism comes amid heightened tensions following the November 2022 peace agreement, which aimed to end a brutal two-year conflict in Tigray. Despite initial progress, accusations of violations have emerged, with the TPLF claiming the federal government is failing to uphold its commitments, particularly regarding humanitarian access and political inclusion. This situation is critical as it threatens to destabilize the fragile peace and exacerbate regional tensions in Ethiopia.

articles.context: في ظل الأوضاع المتوترة في السودان، حيث تشهد البلاد صراعات سياسية وأمنية منذ اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023، تبرز قصة كاتي بيري وجاستن ترودو كرمز للتواصل الثقافي بين الشخصيات العامة في وقت الأزمات. يعكس اهتمام بيري بترودو، الذي كان رئيس وزراء كندا حتى عام 2023، أهمية العلاقات الدولية في تعزيز الروابط الإنسانية، حتى في ظل التحديات التي تواجهها المنطقة.

articles.context: تأتي قصة بسمة بوسيل في سياق متغيرات المشهد الفني في المنطقة، حيث تسعى الفنانات المغربيات إلى تعزيز وجودهن في صناعة الأزياء والموسيقى. في ظل التوترات السياسية والاجتماعية التي تشهدها دول مثل السودان، يمثل نجاح بوسيل نموذجًا ملهمًا للشباب في المنطقة، خاصة بعد اتفاق جوبا للسلام في 2020 الذي يسعى إلى تعزيز الاستقرار والتنمية الثقافية.

articles.title: أول قرار لأشرف حكيمي بعد تأييد إحالته إلى المحاكمة في فرنسا

articles.context: تأتي قضية أشرف حكيمي في وقت حساس للرياضة في فرنسا، حيث تتزايد المخاوف بشأن الاعتداءات الجنسية في المجتمع الرياضي. بعد حركة #MeToo، شهدت البلاد زيادة في الوعي حول هذه القضايا، مما أدى إلى محاكمات بارزة. كما أن حكيمي، الذي يمثل المغرب في كأس العالم، يواجه ضغوطاً إضافية من وسائل الإعلام والجماهير، مما يسلط الضوء على التوترات بين الشهرة والعدالة في السياق الرياضي.

articles.title: Resumption of Talks on Tigray is Urgent, Says EU
articles.summary: The European Union delegation in Ethiopia has urged for the resumption of talks to resolve tensions in Tigray. “A resumption of talks on the concrete implementation of CoHA by all sides is urgent. We fully support the mediation efforts led by the AU’s High Representative President Obasanjo to achieve peace and stability,” said the EU, […]
articles.context: The conflict in Tigray, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), has resulted in a humanitarian crisis and widespread violence. The signing of the Cessation of Hostilities Agreement (CoHA) on November 2, 2022, marked a significant step towards peace, but implementation has stalled. The EU's call for renewed talks highlights the urgency of addressing ongoing tensions and the need for effective mediation by the African Union.

articles.context: تحتفل نوال الزغبي بعيد ميلادها في وقت يشهد فيه العالم العربي، بما في ذلك لبنان، تحديات اقتصادية وسياسية كبيرة. الأزمة الاقتصادية اللبنانية المستمرة منذ 2019 أثرت على صناعة الموسيقى والفنون، مما يجعل نجاح نوال الزغبي في الحفاظ على مكانتها الفنية وسط هذه الظروف أكثر أهمية. كما أن تفاعلها مع جمهورها عبر وسائل التواصل الاجتماعي يعكس قدرة الفنانين على التكيف مع الأزمات والتواصل مع محبيهم.

articles.title: Ugandan ICC Judge Solomy Bossa Takes On Trump Administration Over Controversial Sanctions

articles.title: “Degrees without skills”: Ting Mayai, Prof. John Akec clash over University of Juba’s quality
articles.summary: A public exchange between two of South Sudan’s leading intellectuals has ignited fresh debate over the quality of higher education after the Director General of the National Bureau of Statistics, Dr. Ting Mayai, accused the University of Juba of producing “degrees without skills”, prompting Vice Chancellor Prof. John Akec to fire back that “speeches and […] The post “Degrees without skills”: Ting Mayai, Prof. John Akec clash over University of Juba’s quality appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The University of Juba, established in 1977, has faced significant challenges due to South Sudan's ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011. The clash between Dr. Ting Mayai and Prof. John Akec highlights the urgent discourse on educational reform and workforce readiness, critical for South Sudan's recovery and development.

articles.title: How Editing Alongside CGI Enhances the Magical Feel in Movies
articles.summary: Movies have always been a gateway to worlds beyond our imagination. Whether audiences are watching dragons soar through the skies, enchanted forests come alive, or superheroes wield extraordinary powers, the sense of wonder comes from more than just visual effects. While computer-generated imagery (CGI) often receives the spotlight, film editing plays an equally important role […]

Amid ongoing political tensions and conflict in regions like Tigray, the rise of CGI and advanced editing techniques in Ethiopian cinema offers a means for cultural expression and unity, reflecting the country's diverse narratives and aspirations.

articles.title: U.S. furniture Giant Ashley Opens Second Ethiopian Branch

The new branch, opened in the Gourd Shola area of Addis Ababa, follows the successful opening of the first […]
articles.context: The opening of Ashley Furniture's second showroom in Ethiopia comes amid a recovering economy following the Tigray conflict, which lasted from November 2020 to November 2022. The Ethiopian government is actively seeking foreign investment to bolster economic growth and stability, as outlined in its Homegrown Economic Reform agenda launched in 2020. This expansion by a major U.S. retailer signals renewed confidence in the Ethiopian market and its potential for international business.

articles.title: Ethiopian Cargo Lands 2026 AFLAS Award for Best African Air Cargo Carrier
articles.summary: Ethiopian Cargo and Logistics Services, Africa’s largest air cargo network, has won the 2026 Asia Freight, Logistics and Supply Chain (AFLAS) Award for Best Air Cargo Carrier –

articles.title: Strengthening Families: Lessons from Decades of Alternative Care in Ethiopia

This context highlights the urgent need for effective family-based care solutions amidst the challenges of conflict and displacement.

articles.title: Sudan army chief says no peace talks unless RSF disarms

articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated in April 2023 when clashes broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group formerly allied with the military. articles.summary: The average rate of Ethiopian Birr (ETB) has slightly strengthened against the US dollar in the foreign exchange trading on Tuesday, the results released by the Central Bank
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant economic challenges in recent years, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020, which disrupted trade and investment. The government has been implementing reforms to stabilize the economy, including a managed float of the Birr. The recent FX auction results, showing a slight strengthening of the Birr, come amid ongoing efforts to attract foreign investment and restore economic confidence following the conflict's impact.

articles.title: Explainer: Critical constraints risk further Ebola spread as cases surpass 1,000
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – As the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with cases already surpassing 1,000 and
articles.context: The current Ebola outbreak is exacerbated by ongoing instability in the DRC, particularly in the eastern regions where armed groups, such as the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), have created a volatile environment since 2013. This conflict hampers public health responses and vaccination efforts, complicating containment strategies. Additionally, Uganda's porous borders with the DRC facilitate cross-border transmission, raising concerns about regional health security as both countries grapple with the outbreak's implications.

articles.title: Human Rights Watch Rebukes Repressive Legislation in Tigray
articles.summary: Human Rights Watch has called on regional authorities in Tigray to immediately withdraw a piece of legislation that political observers have described as a “blueprint for authoritarianism.” The international watchdog issued the call after the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) introduced a sweeping security and mobilization proclamation earlier this month, forcing civilians into military service […]
articles.context: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has been at the center of a brutal conflict in Ethiopia since November 2020, when federal forces launched a military offensive against the TPLF, leading to widespread human rights abuses. The recent introduction of the security and mobilization proclamation in October 2023 has raised alarms, as it comes amid ongoing tensions following the November 2022 peace agreement, which aimed to end hostilities but has seen limited progress in restoring stability and governance in Tigray.

articles.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
articles.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.
articles.context: The tragic fire at Utumishi Girls' School on September 26, 2023, has reignited discussions about school safety and accountability in Kenya, particularly in the wake of ongoing debates about the management of educational institutions. The incident follows a series of school-related tragedies in the country, prompting the Ministry of Education to review safety regulations. The decision to charge the students with murder raises questions about juvenile justice and the broader implications for school governance in Kenya.

articles.title: Kenya signs $1.2B deal with Chinese firm to expand Nairobi airport
articles.summary: Project will nearly triple passenger capacity at East Africa's busiest aviation hub
articles.context: Kenya's decision to expand Jomo Kenyatta International Airport comes amid increasing competition from neighboring countries like Ethiopia, which has invested heavily in its own aviation infrastructure. The deal, signed in October 2023 with the Chinese firm China Road and Bridge Corporation, is part of Kenya's Vision 2030 initiative aimed at boosting economic growth and tourism. This expansion is crucial as Kenya seeks to enhance its regional connectivity and secure its position as East Africa's leading aviation hub.

articles.title: EXPLAINER — Sudan war disrupts supplies of gum arabic, a hidden ingredient in everyday products
articles.summary: As fighting persists, Sudan’s globally important gum arabic industry faces mounting risks from insecurity and illicit trade
articles.context: Sudan has been embroiled in conflict since April 2023, when fighting erupted between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread instability. This turmoil threatens the gum arabic industry, which accounts for approximately 80% of the global supply, as production and export routes are disrupted. The ongoing violence not only jeopardizes local livelihoods but also impacts global markets reliant on this key ingredient found in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals.

articles.title: ECMA Approves Registration of Bunna Bank’s Securities
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has finalised the registration of more than 64 million existing and new shares of Bunna Bank S.C, a public notice the regulatory
articles.context: The approval by the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This move is significant as it reflects the government's efforts to stabilize the economy following years of conflict and unrest, particularly in the Tigray region, and to attract foreign investment as part of its broader economic strategy.

articles.title: Ethiopia Selected as Africa’s Meteorology and Hydrology Training Center
articles.summary: It has been officially announced that the Ethiopian Meteorology Institute and Arba Minch University have been formally approved to serve as Regional Training Centers (RTC) for the World Meteorological Organization (WMO). This decision was passed during the 80th session of the World Meteorological Organization’s Executive Council meeting currently being held in Geneva, Switzerland. It was […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's selection as a Regional Training Center comes amid ongoing challenges, including the Tigray conflict that has strained the country's resources and international relations. The decision, made during the WMO's 80th Executive Council meeting in June 2023, highlights Ethiopia's commitment to enhancing regional climate resilience, particularly as the Horn of Africa grapples with severe droughts and climate change impacts. This approval also reflects a shift towards regional cooperation in addressing environmental challenges.

articles.title: Ethiopia Highlights Benefits of New Africa-Wide Biotechnology Information Portal
articles.summary: Ethiopia is set to benefit from improved access to biotechnology and biosafety information following the launch of a new continental digital platform aimed at enhancing transparency and supporting evidence-based decision-making across Africa. The newly introduced Biotech Africa Database, developed by the International Service for the Acquisition of Agri-biotech Applications (ISAAA AfriCenter) and its partners, brings […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's launch of the Biotech Africa Database comes amid ongoing challenges in agricultural productivity exacerbated by climate change and regional conflicts, particularly the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020. With Ethiopia's economy heavily reliant on agriculture, this initiative, supported by ISAAA AfriCenter, aims to enhance food security and promote sustainable agricultural practices, crucial for stabilizing the region and fostering cooperation among neighboring countries like Sudan.

articles.title: China’s new governance pitch aims at the world’s next frontiers
articles.summary: Beijing has a familiar message for a fragmented world: the global system is under strain, but it does not need to be torn down. It needs, China says, to be made more just, more equitable and better suited to the realities of a turbulent century. That is the central argument of a new white paper, […]
articles.context: China's renewed focus on governance comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a two-year civil conflict that ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As Ethiopia seeks to stabilize and rebuild, China's proposal for a more equitable global system resonates, particularly given its investments in African infrastructure through the Belt and Road Initiative. This context highlights China's role as a key player in shaping Ethiopia's political and economic landscape during a critical transitional period.

articles.title: Why Ethiopia Should Study China’s New White Paper
articles.summary: As Ethiopia’s new government takes shape, it should look carefully at China’s latest white paper on global governance — not to copy it wholesale, but to draw practical lessons from a development model that has transformed the country from poverty to power. China’s document, More Just and Equitable Global Governance: China’s Principles, Proposals and Actions, […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The new government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, faces challenges in rebuilding the nation and addressing economic instability. As Ethiopia seeks to enhance its global standing and foster development, examining China's governance model could provide valuable insights for its own political and economic strategies.

articles.title: “They Want to Add Me to a Terrorism File” – Ssenyonyi Alleges Plot Linked to Gen Muhoozi
articles.summary: Leader of Opposition in Parliament Joel Ssenyonyi has claimed that there is a plan to implicate him in The post “They Want to Add Me to a Terrorism File” – Ssenyonyi Alleges Plot Linked to Gen Muhoozi appeared first on The Nile Post .

The backdrop of increasing repression against opposition figures, especially after the 2021 elections, adds gravity to Ssenyonyi's claims, suggesting a potential crackdown on dissent linked to Muhoozi's growing influence within the Ugandan military and political landscape.

articles.title: Guterres Slams Fossil Fuel Windfall Profits, Urges Nations to Tax Extra Gains
articles.summary: The Secretary-General of the United Nations, António Guterres, has strongly condemned the “excessive windfall profits” amassed by globally operating fossil fuel companies. At a time when citizens with modest savings and the working class worldwide are facing skyrocketing energy bills, the Secretary-General stated that it is completely unjust and unacceptable for oil giants to collect […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of a two-year civil conflict that has exacerbated economic challenges, including rising energy costs. The Ethiopian government, facing pressure to stabilize the economy and address public discontent, is particularly sensitive to global energy price fluctuations. Guterres' call for taxing fossil fuel profits resonates in this context, as Ethiopia seeks to balance its energy needs while managing the socioeconomic fallout from the conflict and rising inflation.

articles.title: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
articles.summary: The scale and brutality of the sexual attacks is unprecedented and aimed at terrorising communities, the UN says.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated after the military coup in October 2021, which ousted the transitional government and reignited violence between rival factions, particularly the Sudan Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. The UN's recent findings highlight a disturbing trend of sexual violence being weaponized, particularly in regions like Darfur, where historical grievances and ethnic tensions have fueled brutal attacks since the Darfur conflict began in 2003.

articles.title: Abay Bank to Register as Fifth Institution on ESX as Weekly Trading Volume Hits Historic 1 Billion Birr Milestone
articles.summary: In its ongoing efforts to make the country’s capital market ecosystem accessible, the Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) has announced that it has officially admitted Abay Bank S.C. as its fifth registered financial institution, after the bank successfully met the stringent criteria and legal preparation processes required for the domestic bond and equity trading platform. According […]
articles.context: The admission of Abay Bank to the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing its financial sector, which began in earnest with the 2018 Prosperity Party's rise to power. This move is significant as it follows the signing of the 2020 peace agreement with Tigray, which has allowed for a more stable environment for investment and economic growth, crucial for rebuilding the economy after years of conflict.

articles.title: How Construction Professionals Are Using Short-Form Video to Land High-Paying Engineering Roles
articles.summary: The job market for engineers has been drastically revolutionized. It is evolving into a space where engineers no longer solely rely on resumes to showcase technical abilities; instead they will use animated visual media to provide clear explanations of their technical aptitude. Through mediums such as Instagram Reels, TikTok and LinkedIn short videos will be […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which escalated in November 2020, has significantly impacted the job market, particularly for engineers. As the country seeks to rebuild and modernize its infrastructure post-conflict, the demand for skilled professionals has surged. This shift has prompted engineers to adopt innovative methods, such as short-form video, to stand out in a competitive landscape and secure high-paying roles in a recovering economy.

articles.title: The Passing of a Shepherd of Ideas and a Servant of Humanity
articles.summary: With profound sorrow and deep gratitude, I mourn the passing of my dear friend and brother, Professor Fantu Cheru — an extraordinary Ethiopian and African intellectual whose life embodied the rare harmony between thought and action, scholarship and service, intellect and humility. Fantu’s journey — from the hills of Gondar to the lecture halls of […]
articles.context: Professor Fantu Cheru's passing comes at a time when Ethiopia is grappling with the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. His contributions to peacebuilding and intellectual discourse are particularly poignant as the nation seeks to rebuild and reconcile amidst ongoing ethnic tensions and political instability. Cheru's work in advocating for social justice and development resonates deeply in this challenging context.

articles.title: Sexual violence used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan conflict: UN report
articles.summary: UN rights office verifies 546 cases across country, calls for independent investigations and accountability
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan escalated significantly after the outbreak of violence in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread human rights abuses. The UN report highlights the systematic use of sexual violence as a tactic by armed groups, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis and undermining peace efforts, particularly in light of the 2020 Juba Peace Agreement aimed at ending decades of conflict in the region.

articles.title: Kenya minister halts construction of US-backed Ebola quarantine centre
articles.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.
articles.context: The construction of the US-backed Ebola quarantine center in Kenya has sparked controversy amid ongoing tensions between the Kenyan government and local communities over health infrastructure. In 2021, the Kenyan High Court ruled against the facility, citing concerns over land use and community consent. Aden Duale's defiance of this ruling highlights the ongoing struggle between government authorities and judicial decisions, reflecting broader issues of governance and accountability in Kenya's political landscape.

articles.title: Kenya's health minister apologizes to court over controversial US-backed Ebola facility
articles.summary: Work on disputed health facility stopped, Aden Duale tells court
articles.context: The controversy surrounding the US-backed Ebola facility in Kenya stems from a 2020 agreement between the Kenyan government and the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to enhance disease preparedness. Local communities have raised concerns about land acquisition and potential health risks, leading to protests. The recent halt in construction, prompted by Health Minister Aden Duale's court apology, highlights ongoing tensions between government authorities, local populations, and international partners in health initiatives.

articles.title: South Sudan’s Unfinished Transition: The Search for Common Ground
articles.summary: By: Francis Deng (PhD) & Amir Idris (PhD) South Sudan’s transition remains unfinished, not only because of weak institutions, repeated political crises, and a lack of political will, but also because the country has yet to agree on common ground. At the heart of the problem is a deeper question: what kind of state, system […]
articles.context: South Sudan gained independence from Sudan in 2011 but has been mired in civil conflict since 2013, primarily between factions loyal to President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish a power-sharing government, yet implementation has faltered, leaving the country grappling with ongoing violence, economic instability, and a lack of consensus on governance structures.

articles.title: As Aid Shrinks, Development Leaders Search for a New Model
articles.summary: As foreign aid budgets contract across much of the developed world and debt pressures mount in poorer countries, development leaders meeting in Nairobi this week are debating whether the decades-old aid model can survive a rapidly changing global economy. The debate is unfolding at a two-day conference hosted by the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom, […]
articles.context: The ongoing economic challenges in Kenya, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising global inflation, have led to significant cuts in foreign aid, which traditionally supported development projects. Additionally, the recent droughts and conflicts in neighboring regions, such as Ethiopia's Tigray conflict, have strained resources further. The Nairobi conference comes at a critical time as Kenya seeks to redefine its development strategy amidst these pressures, particularly following the 2021 Nairobi Declaration on Sustainable Development.

articles.title: South Sudan sets December date for long-delayed first-ever election
articles.summary: Plans could yet be scuppered again over logistical issues and continued tensions and violence.
articles.context: South Sudan's first-ever elections, scheduled for December 2023, follow years of civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to a fragile peace agreement in 2018. The Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) aimed to unify rival factions, but ongoing violence and disputes over security arrangements threaten the electoral process. Key actors include President Salva Kiir and opposition leader Riek Machar, whose rivalry continues to fuel instability in the region.

articles.title: Ethiopian Welcomes First Twin Otter Classic 300-G Aircraft from De Havilland Canada

The aircraft will support operations in remote areas, crucial for humanitarian access and local development.

articles.title: شعبة مستوردي الوقود: عجز تمويلي بقيمة 326 مليون دولار يفاقم أزمة النقد الأجنبي في السودان

articles.title: لجنة المعلمين السودانيين تعلن بداية تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم
articles.summary: قالت لجنة المعلمين السودانيين إن تنفيذ الإضراب في ولاية الخرطوم بدأ اليوم وفق الجدول المعلن من لجنة الإضراب، مشيرةً إلى استمراره حتى يوم غدٍ الثلاثاء. وأفادت اللجنة في منشور على “فيسبوك”، أنّ اللجنة العليا للإضراب ستعقد اجتماعها يوم الجمعة المقبل لتقييم الخطوة الحالية ودراسة المستجدات واتخاذ ما تراه مناسباً من قرارات وخطوات قادمة. articles.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
articles.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.
articles.context: Martha Karua, a prominent Kenyan lawyer and former justice minister, has been actively involved in Ugandan politics, particularly in defending opposition figures like Kizza Besigye, who has faced multiple arrests and trials under President Yoweri Museveni's regime. The ongoing political tension in Uganda, marked by Besigye's treason trial that began in 2021, underscores the challenges faced by opposition leaders in the region, highlighting the fragile state of democracy and human rights in East Africa.

articles.title: بشير مرسال يوقف موظفين بجنوب دارفور

articles.title: Why Sustained Support for Refugees in Gambella Matters More Than Ever?
articles.summary: By Charles Ossey At just one month old, NyapilikaJany was fighting for her life in Jewi Refugee Camp in Ethiopia’s Gambella Region. Her mother, Nyabel Deng, who fled conflict and sought refuge in Ethiopia in […]

articles.title: المعلمون يواصلون الإضراب في كسلا وإضراب جديد في الخرطوم
